description.value: This code system includes a selection of Adjudication Value Codes which convey the payers assessment of the item provided in the claim under the terms of the patient’s insurance coverage.

This value set encompasses all adjudication codes used by the payer to convey their assessment of submitted claim items. It includes codes for non-covered charges, allowed amounts, provider payments, and other critical financial and administrative decisions made during the adjudication process under the patient's insurance coverage.
The base number of units assigned to the line item anesthesia procedure on the carrier claim (non-DMERC).

name.value: ANSI Group Code - Claim Adjustment Group Code

description.value: The Group Code is combined with the ANSI reason code to demonstrate who has financial responsibility for the amount.

name.value: ANSI Reason Code - Claim Adjustment Reason Code

description.value: A code used to identify the detailed reason an adjustment was made (e.g. reason for denial or reducing payment).
Monthly Part A and/or Part B entitlement indicator CodeSystem.
Monthly Part A and/or Part B entitlement indicator.
The contract number associated with the beneficiary's Medicare coverage.
A code identifying the type of Medicare coverage for a beneficiary.
A code identifying the type of Medicare coverage for a beneficiary.
AN IDENTIFIER INDICATING WHETHER DISABILITY IS A REASON FOR A BENEFICIARYS ENTITLEMENT TO MEDICARE.
An code indicating if the Beneficiary is entitled to medicare due to a disability.
A code identifying the dual eligibility status for a beneficiary.
A code identifying the dual eligibility status for a beneficiary.
A code identifying the dual eligibility type for a beneficiary.
A code identifying the dual eligibility type for a beneficiary.
The reason for a beneficiary's enrollment in Part A/Part B benefits.
CODES AND DESCRIPTIONS THAT IDENTIFY THE REASON A BENEFICIARY ENROLLED IN A SERVICE ELECTION.
A code identifying the basis in determining a beneficiary's entitlement to Medicare benefits.
The reason for entitlement or termination of a beneficiary's benefits during a period of coverage.
The reason for entitlement or termination of a beneficiary's benefits during a period of coverage.
A beneficiary's ESRD Status indicator.
A beneficiary's ESRD Status indicator.
Indicates the copayment level for Low Income Subsidy (LIS) beneficiaries
Codes indicating the copayment level for Low Income Subsidy (LIS) beneficiaries
Indicates whether the beneficiary has effective Low Income Subsidy (LIS) coverage
Codes indicating the effective status of Low Income Subsidy (LIS) for a beneficiary

name.value: Beneficiary LIS Part D Premium Percentage

description.value: Indicates the percentage of Part D premium covered by Low Income Subsidy (LIS)
The reason for a beneficiary's entitlement to Medicare.
A code identifying the reason for a beneficiary's entitlement to Medicare benefits.
Indicates the patient’s discharge status as of the Claim Through Date
The PBP (Prescription Benefit Plan) number associated with the beneficiary's Medicare coverage.
A switch indicating enrollment by a beneficiary who has an employer subsidy.
A switch indicating enrollment by a beneficiary who has an employer subsidy.
This profile builds on top of the CARIN for Blue Button (C4BB) Explanation of Benefit Pharmacy profile to represent pharmacy claims within BFD.
This field indicates whether the drug is a brand or generic.

name.value: Carrier Claim Provider Assignment Indicator Switch

description.value: Variable indicates whether or not the provider accepts assignment for the non-institutional claim.

name.value: Carrier Line Health Professional Shortage Area (HPSA)/Scarcity Indicator Code

description.value: The code used to track health professional shortage area (HPSA) and physician scarcity bonus payments on carrier claims.
Code indicating the units associated with services needing unit reporting on the line item for the carrier claim (non-DMERC).

name.value: Carrier Line Reduced Payment Physician Assistant Code

description.value: The code on the carrier (non-DMERC) line item that identifies the line items that have been paid a reduced fee schedule amount (65%, 75% or 85%) because a physician's assistant performed the service.
Code indicating the units associated with services needing unit reporting on the line item for the carrier claim (non-DMERC).
Code identifying the type of provider or supplier furnishing the service for this line item on the carrier claim. Also used for conveying supplier types.

This variable indicates whether the PDE occurred within the catastrophic benefit phase of the Medicare Part D benefit, according to the source PDE.
When the value equals C (above attachment point), then the PDE is in the catastrophic phase. When the value equals A (attachment point), the PDE has caused the beneficiary to move into the catastrophic phase (i.e., this is the 'triggering' PDE).
Tag codes indicating whether or not a claim has been fully adjudicated. More specifically, whether or not a claim has been released to the National Claims History.
DOMAIN OF CLAIM ADJUSTMENT TYPES, INCLUDING DENOTING ORIGINAL CLAIMS
Indicates the source of the beneficiary’s referral for admission or visit.
Indicates the type and priority of inpatient services.
Claim Benefit Enhancement Codes
Claim Benefit Enhancement Code Switch.

The second digit of the type of bill (TOB2) is used to indicate with greater specificity where the service was provided (e.g., a department within a hospital). In FHIR, CMS concatenates the first and second digits.
The first digit of the type of bill (TOB1) is used to identify the type of facility that provided care to the beneficiary (e.g., hospital or SNF).
It indicates the sequence of the claim in the beneficiary's current episode of care (e.g., interim or voided).
A number assigned by CMS identifying a MAC authorized to process Medicare claims.
CODE INDICATING WHETHER THE REVENUE CENTER CHARGES ARE SUBJECT TO DEDUCTIBLE AND/OR COINSURANCE.

name.value: Claim Diagnosis Related Group Outlier Stay Code

description.value: On an institutional claim, the code that indicates the beneficiary stay under the prospective payment system (PPS) which, although classified into a specific diagnosis related group, has an unusually long length (day outlier) or exceptionally high cost (cost outlier).

name.value: Claim Diagnosis Related Group Outlier Stay Code

description.value: On an institutional claim, the code that indicates the beneficiary stay under the prospective payment system (PPS) which, although classified into a specific diagnosis related group, has an unusually long length (day outlier) or exceptionally high cost (cost outlier).
Indicates the status of prescription fulfillment.
Code indicating the disposition or outcome of the processing of the claim record.
The type of action requested by the intermediary to be taken on an institutional claim.
The count of the total units associated with services needing unit reporting such as transportation, miles, anesthesia time units, number of services, volume of oxygen or blood units.
The number used to identify the prescription order number for drugs and biologicals purchased through the competitive acquisition program (CAP).
The code used to identify those Home Health PPS claims that have 4 visits or less in a 60-day episode.

name.value: Claim Near Line Record Identification Code

description.value: A CODE IDENTIFYING THE CLAIM RECORD TYPE BEING PROCESSED.
THE REASON THAT NO MEDICARE PAYMENT IS MADE FOR SERVICES ON AN INSTITUTIONAL CLAIM.
THE DOMAIN OF OTAF INDICATOR CODES
A code reported by the provider that indicates the specific type of claim
A PAYMENT STATUS OF A CLAIM.
The code on a non-institutional claim indicating who receives payment or if the claim was denied.
The code indicating the place of service, as defined in the Medicare Carrier Manual, for this line item on the non-institutional claim.
The code indicating whether or not: (1) the claim is from the prospective payment system (PPS), and/or (2) the beneficiary is a deemed insured MQGE (Medicare Qualified Government Employee)
Extension for expressing the tax number of a provider on a line item.
Code indicating the type of claim record being processed with respect to payment (debit/credit indicator; interim/final indicator).
The code used to identify those Home Health PPS claims that have 4 visits or less in a 60-day episode.
This code indicates information about how a claim should be processed.
Domain of Revenue Center Codes

name.value: Claim Revenue Center Packaging Indicator Code

description.value: THE DOMAIN OF CLAIM REVENUE PACKAGE INDICATOR CODES.
Item-level discount indicator code.
THE DOMAIN OF CLAIM REVENUE DISCOUNT INDICATOR CODES.
AN IDENTIFIER INDICATING WHERE THE SOURCE OF THE CLAIM DATA IN THE INTEGRATED DATA REPOSITORY (IDR) ORIGINATED FROM.
The current status information for the pending and paid claims currently in the system. Augmented to derive using system type + location code + status code.
Code system for claim submission format codes indicating the method or format used to submit the claim.

name.value: Claim Submit Format Code Value Set

description.value: The format of the claim as submitted by the provider.
THE DOMAIN OF CLAIM SUPPLIER TYPE CODES.
CA CODE IDENTIFYING THE SOURCE AND TYPE OF CLAIM SUBMITTED THROUGH THE MEDICARE OR MEDICAID PROGRAM.
A value set identifying the source and type of claim submitted through the Medicare or Medicaid program.
This field identifies the payment for disproportionate share hospitals (DSH). It represents the uncompensated care amount of the payment.
Code indicating the type of service, as defined in the CMS Medicare Carrier Manual, for this line item on the non-institutional claim.
This field indicates whether or not the dispensed drug was compounded or mixed.

This variable is the segment number that CMS assigns to identify a geographic market segment or subdivision of a Part D plan; the segment number allows you to determine the market area covered by the plan.
Indicates the prescriber's instructions regarding generic substitution or how those instructions were followed

This field indicates whether or not the drug is covered by Medicare Part D. The Part D benefit does not cover all FDA-approved drugs. However, plan sponsors can offer an 'enhanced' benefit package that covers non-Part D drugs, such as over-the-counter medications.
Tag codes indicating whether or not a claim has been marked as final action.
The type of test that was performed - hematocrit or hemoglobin.
The code on the carrier (non-DMERC) line item that identifies the line items that have been paid a reduced fee schedule amount (65%, 75% or 85%) because a physician's assistant performed the service.
Code indicating the type of service, as defined in the CMS Medicare carrier manual, for this line item on the non-institutional claim.

The code indicating that the amount shown in the payment field on the non-institutional line item represents either 80% or 100% of the allowed charges less any deductible, or 100% limitation of liability only.
Code denoting the carrier-specific locality used for pricing the service for this line item on the carrier claim (non-DMERC).
Code denoting the carrier-specific locality used for pricing the service for this line item on the carrier claim (non-DMERC).

The code specifying a federal non-Medicare program or other source that has primary responsibility for the payment of the Medicare beneficiary's medical bills relating to the line-item service on the non-institutional claim.

name.value: Line Primary Payer Code (if not Medicare)

description.value: The code specifying a federal non-Medicare program or other source that has primary responsibility for the payment of the Medicare beneficiary's medical bills relating to the line item service on the non-institutional claim.
The USPS state code associated with the beneficiary's dual eligible status.
A code identifying the reason for a beneficiary's entitlement to Medicare benefits.
A SWITCH INDICATING WHETHER OR NOT A MANAGED CARE ORGANIZATION (MCO) HAS PAID THE PROVIDER FOR AN INSTITUTIONAL CLAIM.
A CODE IDENTIFYING THE COVERAGE TYPE OF THE PRIMARY PAYER WHEN MEDICARE IS THE SECONDARY PAYER AS DEFINED BY THE COMMON WORKING FILE (CWF).
A code defining the type of claim record being processed.

This is one component of the total amount that is payable on prospective payment system (PPS) claims and reflects the DSH (disproportionate share hospital) payments for operating expenses (such as labor) for the claim.
This is one component of the total amount that is payable on PPS claims, and reflects the IME (indirect medical education) payments for operating expenses (such as labor) for the claim.

This variable indicates where the beneficiary lived when the prescription was filled, as reported on the PDE record. CMS requires Part D plans to maintain pharmacy networks that are sufficient to ensure convenient access to Medicare beneficiaries – including retail, home infusion, and long-term care pharmacies. This variable is designed to help CMS and plan sponsors monitor beneficiary access to a range of 'in-network' pharmacies.
EFFECTIVE WITH NCH VERSION H, THE CODE ON AN INPATIENT/SNF AND HOSPICE CLAIM, INDICATING WHETHER THE BENEFICIARY WAS DISCHARGED, DIED OR STILL A PATIENT (USED FOR INTERNAL CWFMQA EDITING PURPOSES.)

The code indicating that the amount shown in the payment field on the non-institutional line item represents either 80% or 100% of the allowed charges less any deductible, or 100% limitation of liability only.

The type of pharmacy used. This variable indicates the type of pharmacy that dispensed the prescription, as recorded on the PDE. CMS requires Part D plans to maintain pharmacy networks that are sufficient to ensure access to Medicare beneficiaries – including retail, home infusion, and long-term care pharmacies. Plan sponsors have contracts with pharmacies to provide 'in-network' services.
This variable indicates whether the prescription was transmitted as an electronic prescription, by phone, by fax, or as a written paper copy.
The code on a non-institutional claim indicating to whom payment was made or if the claim was denied.
The code on a non-institutional claim indicating to whom payment was made or if the claim was denied.
Indicates the type of number used to identify the provider.
Code indicating whether or not a provider is participating (accepting assignment) for this line-item service on the non-institutional claim.
Code indicating whether or not a provider is participating (accepting assignment) for this line-item service on the non-institutional claim.
CMS (previously called HCFA) specialty code used for pricing the line-item service on the non-institutional claim.
Code identifying the type of provider furnishing the service for this line item on the claim. Also used for conveying supplier types.
The code on the carrier (non-DMERC) line item that identifies the line items that have been paid a reduced fee schedule amount (65%, 75% or 85%) because a physician's assistant performed the service.

name.value: Revenue Center Obligation to Accept As Full Payment Code

description.value: Revenue Center Obligation to Accept As Full Payment Code
The code used to identify those services that are packaged/bundled with another service.

name.value: Revenue Center Payment Method Indicator Code

description.value: Extension used to provide the variable that indicates how the service listed on the revenue center record was priced for payment purposes.

name.value: Revenue Center Payment Method Indicator Code

description.value: Extension used to provide the code used to identify how the service is priced for payment.

name.value: Revenue Center Payment Method Indicator Code

description.value: THE DOMAIN OF CLAIM PAYMENT METHOD CODES.
THE DOMAIN OF CLAIM REVENUE CENTER STATUS CODES.
Switch indicating whether or not the line-item service on the non-institutional claim is subject to a deductible.
Switch indicating whether or not the line-item service on the non-institutional claim is subject to a deductible.
Switch indicating whether or not the line-item service on the non-institutional claim is subject to a deductible.
Tag codes indicating whether or not a claim comes from a CMS Shared System or the CMS National Claims History.

For beneficiaries living in long-term care (LTC) facilities, this variable indicates how many days' supply of the medication was dispensed by the long-term care pharmacy and provides some details about the dispensing event.
This variable is only populated when beneficiary lives in an LTC facility (i.e., when the CLM_PTNT_RSDNC_CD variable equals 03)
The type of DMERC supplier.
A selection of codes that provide supporting information about the claim.

This value set provides a comprehensive collection of codes used to identify and categorize various types of supporting information provided on insurance claims. This includes categories such as physical examinations, diagnoses, administrative metadata, and other relevant contexts necessary for claim adjudication.
